Use the message tool if you need to notify the user directly with an explicit target. If you do not send directly, your final plain-text reply will be delivered automatically.
I'll run a sync to GitHub to save version history. Let me first check the workspace state and any git repos.

arguments.command:
for dir in /Users/landonking/Projects/business-builder /Users/landonking/Projects/openclaw-business /Users/landonking/Projects/simple-parameter-program; do
  if [ -d "$dir/.git" ]; then
    echo "=== $dir ==="
    cd "$dir" && git status --short && git remote -v | head -2 && git log --oneline -1
  else
    echo "=== $dir === (no git repo)"
  fi
  echo ""
done

arguments.command: cd ~/.openclaw/workspace && echo "Last commit:" && git log -1 --format="%H %ai %s" && echo "---" && echo "Stashed:" && git stash list 2>/dev/null | head -3

content.text: Everything up-to-date
Now let me also back up the OpenClaw config and any other important files that aren't in the workspace repo.

arguments.command:
# Check if openclaw.json and other config files are tracked in workspace
cd ~/.openclaw/workspace && git ls-files | grep -E "openclaw\.json|config" | head -10; echo "---"
# Also check memory files
ls -la memory/ 2>/dev/null | tail -5
